message:
feat(ui): add data-stream border animations and flickering status to site header

Enhances the scrolled-state site header with several new visual effects
that reinforce the "living system" Frankenstein aesthetic:

NeuralPulse containment:
  - The NeuralPulse effect is now wrapped in a dedicated overflow-hidden
    container with rounded-full, matching the header's pill shape. This
    pairs with the FrankenContainer skin-layer refactor: since the
    outer container no longer clips overflow, the header needs its own
    clipping boundary for the pulse animation.
  - NeuralPulse receives an explicit green color (#22c55e) matching
    the site's primary accent.

Border data streams:
  - Two motion.div elements create thin gradient streaks that travel
    along the top and bottom edges of the header in opposite directions
    (3s and 4s periods, staggered by 1s delay). The gradients blend
    from transparent through green → white → green → transparent,
    simulating data packets flowing through the header's border.
  - A subtle green inner glow (bg-green-500/[0.02] with inset shadow)
    adds depth to the scrolled state.

FrankenBolt theming:
  - Logo FrankenBolts now receive color="#22c55e" for green-themed
    bolt accents, consistent with per-tool coloring used elsewhere.
  - Logo container changed to overflow-visible so bolts render
    outside the boundary.

SYSTEM_ALIVE flicker:
  - The status text is wrapped in motion.span with a keyframe opacity
    animation [1, 0.4, 1, 0.8, 1] over 4 seconds that simulates
    a terminal display flicker / heartbeat effect.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
